    38→- [x] Docstring references source papers (`[ErSz83]`, `[Bl25]`).
    39→
    40→### `answer()` usage
    41→
    42→The original problem is a direct assertion — "for every ε > 0 there exists c > 0 such that max(|A+A|, |A·A|) ≥ c·|A|^{2−ε}" — so **Case 3** applies.
    43→
    44→- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper). Correct for a direct assertion.
    45→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ ε`, `∃ c`, `∀ A`) are part of the proposition itself, not theorem parameters. Correct.

    48→
    49→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
    50→- [x] Value is valid (`research open`).
    51→- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

    58→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    59→- [x] Both 5 (Combinatorics) and 11 (Number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes. Appropriate for the Erdős–Szemerédi sum-product problem.
    60→
    61→## 9. Proof Bodies
    62→

    64→- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` (none present).
    65→- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
    66→- [x] No `native_decide`.

    69→

    71→
    72→## 11. Code Quality

    74→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines in the theorem statement use standard 4-space alignment for readability, `sorry` is indented 2 spaces from `by`).

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/52.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–32).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 52`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/52`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the sum-product conjecture.
- [x] Bibliography entries present for `[ErSz83]` and `[Bl25]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos52 ... end Erdos52`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos52`).
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block (lines 34, 36).

## 5. Custom Definitions

N/A — no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_52` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line** (line 50: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`).
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 40–48).
- [x] Docstring references source papers (`[ErSz83]`, `[Bl25]`).

### `answer()` usage

The original problem is a direct assertion — "for every ε > 0 there exists c > 0 such that max(|A+A|, |A·A|) ≥ c·|A|^{2−ε}" — so **Case 3** applies.

- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper). Correct for a direct assertion.
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ ε`, `∃ c`, `∀ A`) are part of the proposition itself, not theorem parameters. Correct.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is valid (`research open`).
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 11).
- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: 5 < 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (Combinatorics) and 11 (Number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes. Appropriate for the Erdős–Szemerédi sum-product problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses `by sorry` (multi-line form: `by\n  sorry` at lines 55–56, which is acceptable per checklist §9).
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` (none present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A — no variants present. The file contains only the single main conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines in the theorem statement use standard 4-space alignment for readability, `sorry` is indented 2 spaces from `by`).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `ε`, `ℝ`, `ℤ`, `≥`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] All lines reasonably short (well under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review. Must be verified separately.

---

**Verdict:** All checkable items pass. The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant with the style guidelines. The only unchecked box is build verification, which requires running `lake build`.

content.text: The review passed — all checkable items pass with the only unchecked box being build verification (which is expected). No changes needed to `deepmind/52.lean`.
